title: Caching Strategies Quiz
description: Test your knowledge about the critical role of caching strategies and their implemention logic.

questions.question: Which of these is the most common server-side caching strategy in modern web development?
What's a primary benefit of the 'Cache-Aside' strategy?
options: It's more secure than other strategies.
It's resilient to cache failures; the application can always fall back to the primary database.
It automatically generates a thumbail for each image.
It's the only way to store data in a database.

questions.question: In a 'Write-Through' caching strategy, what happens when a user updates their profile information?
options: Both the cache and the primary database are updated simultaneously.

questions.question: What is the main risk of using a 'Write-Behind' (Write-Back) strategy?
options: It makes the application too slow.
Risk of data loss if the cache server crashes before it asynchronous updates the database.

questions.question: What is 'TTL' in the context of caching?
options: Time-To-Live; the duration for which a piece of data is considered fresh in the cache.
A way to automatically compress files.
